title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the force on the rider by the sled, we need to consider the components of acceleration and the mass of the astronaut.

title: Using Newton's Second Law
text: Newton's second law states that force is the product of mass and acceleration. We need to find the net acceleration first.

title: Calculating Net Acceleration
text: The net acceleration can be found using the Pythagorean theorem since the accelerations are perpendicular: $$a_{net}=\sqrt{5^2+{3.8}^2}$$.

title: Calculate Net Acceleration
text: Calculate the net acceleration using the formula: $$a_{net}=\sqrt{5^2+{3.8}^2}$$.

title: Finding the Force
text: Now that we have the net acceleration, use $$F=m a$$ to find the force.

title: Calculate the Force
text: Use the formula $$F=m a$$ with $$m=120$$ and $$a=6.3$$ to find the force.
